questions:
Locate the official Sales & Refund Terms and Conditions for the Apple Online Store region that lists '1800-692-7753' as the contact center number and explicitly states that refunds are only remitted to a domestic bank account where the holder's name matches the payor. Based on this specific policy document, provide a detailed comparison of the return constraints for the following three purchase categories: 

1. **ZEISS Optical Inserts**: Distinguish between the return location/method permitted for 'Prescription' inserts versus 'Readers'.
2. **Volume Purchases**: Define the specific criteria for a 'volume' purchase (quantity threshold) and state the return deadline (in days) and the percentage of the restocking fee applied.
3. **iPhone Returns**: Explain the policy regarding the status of the wireless service agreement and associated fees when an iPhone is returned.

Synthesize these details into a comprehensive guide.

Based on the Apple Consumer Store Sales & Refunds Policy for Singapore and the general Apple Website Terms of Use, provide a consolidated legal summary. First, define what constitutes a 'volume' purchase under the Singapore store's specific terms, and detail the return deadline (in days) and the restocking fee percentage applicable to such purchases. Second, citing the general 'Ownership of Site' Terms of Use, specify the governing law jurisdiction for dispute resolution and the exact monetary limitation of liability clause (including the specific fallback US dollar amount).

rounds.question:
Locate the official Sales & Refund Terms and Conditions for the Apple Online Store region that lists '1800-692-7753' as the contact center number and explicitly states that refunds are only remitted to a domestic bank account where the holder's name matches the payor. Based on this specific policy document, provide a detailed comparison of the return constraints for the following three purchase categories: 

1. **ZEISS Optical Inserts**: Distinguish between the return location/method permitted for 'Prescription' inserts versus 'Readers'.
2. **Volume Purchases**: Define the specific criteria for a 'volume' purchase (quantity threshold) and state the return deadline (in days) and the percentage of the restocking fee applied.
3. **iPhone Returns**: Explain the policy regarding the status of the wireless service agreement and associated fees when an iPhone is returned.

Synthesize these details into a comprehensive guide.
checklist_width: ZEISS Prescription Inserts: Cannot be returned to an Apple Store location (Apple accepts returns on behalf of ZEISS via online/phone).
ZEISS Readers: Can be returned to an Apple Store location.
Volume Purchase Definition: Aggregate of more than 4 items per order or across multiple orders from the same product category.
Volume Return Deadline: Must be returned within 7 days of receipt.
Volume Restocking Fee: Subject to a 25% restocking fee per item.
iPhone Wireless Policy: Returning the iPhone does not automatically cancel/reset the wireless account; the user remains responsible for fees/agreement.
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Apple Online Store Singapore (or Singapore Consumer Store).
Logic Proof: The phone number '1800-692-7753' and the 'domestic bank account' refund restriction are unique identifiers for the Singapore region policy.
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep Logic' by masking the target entity (Apple Store Singapore) behind unique identifiers found in the text (phone number and specific bank refund clause). It requires 'Wide Aggregation' by forcing the agent to extract and compare distinct rules for three separate product categories (ZEISS, Volume, iPhone) found in different sections of the identified policy document.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Singapore Consumer Store Thanks for shopping at Apple. We appreciate the fact that you like to buy the cool stuff we build. We also want to make sure you have a rewarding expe
rounds.answer_a:
The Apple Online Store Sales & Refund Terms for Singapore (contact: 1800-692-7753) specify that refunds are remitted only to domestic bank accounts matching the payor's name.

**ZEISS Optical Inserts Returns:**
Prescription ZEISS Optical Inserts must be returned to Apple Store locations or via mail through Apple's standard return process. Readers (non-prescription) can be returned using the same channels as prescription inserts - either at Apple Store locations or through mail returns. Both types follow the standard 14-day return window from delivery date.

**Volume Purchase Returns:**
Volume purchases are defined as orders of 10 or more units of the same product. These purchases have a 30-day return deadline from the invoice date. A 15% restocking fee applies to all volume purchase returns, deducted from the refund amount. Business customers must coordinate returns through Apple's Business Team rather than standard consumer channels.

**iPhone Return Policy:**
When returning an iPhone, the wireless service agreement status depends on the carrier activation. If the iPhone was activated with a carrier during purchase, customers must contact their carrier separately to cancel the service agreement. Apple's return doesn't automatically terminate carrier contracts. Early termination fees from the carrier may apply independently of Apple's return policy. Unlocked iPhones purchased without carrier activation have no service agreement implications upon return.

All returns require original packaging, accessories, and documentation. Products must be in resalable condition. Custom-configured products and engraved items cannot be returned unless defective under warranty terms.

## References
[1] Apple Sales & Refund Terms - Singapore: https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[2] Apple Store Sales Terms and Conditions: https://www.apple.com/legal/sales-support/sales-policies/retail_sg.html
[3] Apple Business Terms: https://www.apple.com/sg/business/shop/
rounds.answer_b:
The Apple Online Store contact number 1800-692-7753 is associated with Apple’s U.S. customer service. However, the specific policy requiring refunds to be remitted only to a domestic bank account where the holder's name matches the payor does not appear in publicly available U.S. Sales & Refund Terms. Apple’s standard U.S. refund policy allows returns within 14 days of delivery for most products, with refunds issued to the original payment method, not direct bank accounts, and no explicit mention of name-matching domestic bank requirements exists in official documents [1].

Regarding ZEISS Optical Inserts, Apple does not sell ZEISS inserts directly through its online store, nor are specific return methods for prescription versus reader inserts outlined in Apple’s return policies [2]. For Volume Purchases, Apple defines a bulk purchase generally as five or more of the same item, qualifying for a Volume Purchase Program (VPP) through the Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager. These purchases are typically non-returnable and non-refundable once processed [3]. No restocking fee applies because returns are generally not permitted.

For iPhone returns, Apple allows return within 14 days if the device is in new, unused condition with original packaging. If the iPhone is activated, it may still be returned as long as it meets condition requirements. The wireless carrier service agreement is separate; Apple does not manage service contracts. Customers must contact their carrier directly to cancel service and avoid ongoing charges. Return to Apple does not automatically cancel the carrier contract [4].

Given the absence of the cited bank account policy in official U.S. terms, and the lack of ZEISS insert handling details, this guide is based on current publicly available U.S. policies from Apple, which do not align fully with the described conditions.

## References  
[1] Apple Refund and Exchange Policy - https://www.apple.com/legal/sales-support/  
[2] Apple Store Product Listings - https://www.apple.com/shop  
[3] Apple Business Manager Help - https://support.apple.com/apple-business-manager  
[4] iPhone Returns and Carrier Service - https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201434
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Agent A correctly identified the core entity (Apple Online Store Singapore) based on the unique phone number and bank account refund policy provided in the prompt. It successfully navigated the 'Deep Logic' check. However, Agent A is a 'Flawed Winner' because it hallucinated significant details regarding the specific constraints: it provided incorrect numbers for Volume Purchases (claiming 10 units/15% fee/30 days instead of the actual >4 units/25% fee/7 days) and incorrect instructions for ZEISS Prescription inserts (claiming they can be returned to stores, when the policy states they cannot). 

Agent B failed the 'Deep Logic' check entirely, incorrectly identifying the number as U.S.-based and providing irrelevant U.S. policy information that explicitly contradicted the prompt's constraints. Agent A wins because it found the correct region and provided the correct source link, whereas Agent B provided information for the wrong continent.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.question: Based on the Apple Consumer Store Sales & Refunds Policy for Singapore and the general Apple Website Terms of Use, provide a consolidated legal summary. First, define what constitutes a 'volume' purchase under the Singapore store's specific terms, and detail the return deadline (in days) and the restocking fee percentage applicable to such purchases. Second, citing the general 'Ownership of Site' Terms of Use, specify the governing law jurisdiction for dispute resolution and the exact monetary limitation of liability clause (including the specific fallback US dollar amount).
checklist_width: Volume Purchase Definition: Aggregate of more than 4 items per order or across multiple orders (from same product category)
Governing Law Jurisdiction: State of California / United States
Liability Limit: Greater of (1) total subscription/feature fees paid in the prior six months or (2) US$100.00
checklist_depth: Target Entity 1: Singapore Consumer Store Sales & Refund Terms (identified via 'Singapore' and 'Volume' logic)
Target Entity 2: Ownership of Site; Agreement to Terms of Use (identified via 'Website Terms' and 'Liability' logic)
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to locate the specific regional policy (Singapore) and extract conditional definitions for 'volume' purchases that differ from standard terms. It then requires Wide Aggregation to combine these specific commercial terms with legal liability and jurisdiction details found in a separate, general 'Terms of Use' document.
source_nodes: Legal - Website Terms of Use - Apple
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: SALES & REFUND TERMS AND CONDITIONS (“TERMS”) Singapore Consumer Store Thanks for shopping at Apple. We appreciate the fact that you like to buy the cool stuff we build. We al
rounds.answer_a:
Under the Apple Consumer Store Sales & Refunds Policy for Singapore, volume purchases are specifically defined as orders containing 10 or more units of the same product [1]. These bulk orders are subject to distinct return conditions that differ from standard consumer purchases. Volume purchases must be returned within 30 days from the invoice date, providing a longer window than the standard 14-day return period for regular consumer purchases [1]. Additionally, all volume purchase returns incur a mandatory 15% restocking fee, which is automatically deducted from the refund amount [1]. This restocking fee applies regardless of the product condition or reason for return, making volume purchases a more restrictive category for returns.

The Apple Website Terms of Use establish comprehensive legal frameworks for dispute resolution and liability limitations. All disputes arising from the use of Apple's website and services are governed by the laws of the State of California, United States, without regard to conflict of law provisions [2]. Any legal proceedings must be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in Santa Clara County, California [2]. The Terms explicitly limit Apple's total liability to the greater of the amount actually paid by the user for the specific product or service in question, or US$100 (one hundred US dollars) [2]. This limitation applies to all claims whether based on warranty, contract, negligence, strict liability, or any other legal theory, even if Apple has been advised of the possibility of such damages [2]. The US$100 fallback amount serves as the minimum threshold when no specific purchase amount can be determined.

## References
[1] Apple Store Sales & Refunds Policy - Singapore: https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[2] Apple Website Terms of Use: https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/terms/site.html
rounds.answer_b:
A 'volume' purchase under the Apple Online Store for Singapore is defined as an order of five or more units of the same item, typically managed through the Apple Business Manager or Apple Education Store, with specific terms differing from standard consumer purchases [1]. For such volume purchases, Apple generally does not accept returns or provide refunds unless required by law, and there is no defined return window or restocking fee in the consumer-facing policy, as these transactions are considered final and non-refundable upon processing [2]. Standard consumer returns for non-volume purchases in Singapore are allowed within 14 days of delivery, but this does not typically extend to bulk orders.

Under the general Apple Website Terms of Use, the governing law for dispute resolution is the laws of Singapore, and any legal action arising from or relating to the use of Apple’s website or purchases made through the Singapore store shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Singapore [3]. The limitation of liability clause states that Apple’s total liability arising out of or in connection with the website or its services shall not exceed the amount paid by the user for accessing the service, or if the user accessed the site for free, then the liability shall not exceed one hundred United States dollars (US$100) as a maximum cap for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages [4].

## References  
[1] Apple Singapore Online Store - Purchase Policies - https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/sales-support/  
[2] Apple Business Manager - Volume Purchase Program - https://support.apple.com/en-sg/HT205924  
[3] Apple Website Terms of Use (Singapore) - https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/internet-services/terms/site.html  
[4] Apple Limitation of Liability Clause - https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/terms/site.html
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed significantly on the first part of the query (Singapore Volume Purchase Policy). Both hallucinated the specific numbers defined in the Ground Truth (Agent A invented 10 units/30 days/15% fee; Agent B invented a 'no return' policy). However, Agent A wins because it correctly answered the second part of the query regarding the General Terms of Use (identifying California and the US$100 limit). Agent B failed the second part by incorrectly assuming Singapore law governed the general website terms, falling into a localization trap.
